encoder.c(3) Library Functions Manual encoder.c(3)

NAME

encoder.c - FFmpeg HEVC encoder for ASCII art frames.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct h265_encoder

Typedefs


typedef struct h265_encoder h265_encoder_t

Functions


h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)
void h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t *ascii_data, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_get_stats (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint64_t *total_frames, uint64_t *keyframes, uint32_t *avg_bitrate)

Detailed Description

FFmpeg HEVC encoder for ASCII art frames.

Definition in file encoder.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct h265_encoder h265_encoder_t

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t * ascii_data, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t * output_size)

Encode an ASCII art frame

The encoder automatically detects size changes and reinitializes when needed. The output packet includes frame dimensions so decoder can reconfigure.

Parameters

encoder Encoder handle
width Current frame width (characters)
height Current frame height (characters)
ascii_data Raw ASCII character grid (width × height bytes)
output_buf Buffer for encoded output
output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual encoded size

Returns

Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success)

OUTPUT PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][x265_data...]

Definition at line 285 of file encoder.c.

286                                                                         {
287   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_1] Starting h265_encode %ux%u", width, height);
288   if (!encoder || !ascii_data || !output_buf || !output_size) {
289     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid encoder arguments");
290   }
291 
292   if (*output_size < 5) {
293     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
294   }
295 
296   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_2] Reconfigure %ux%u -> %ux%u", encoder->current_width, encoder->current_height, width, height);
297   asciichat_error_t result = h265_encoder_reconfigure(encoder, width, height);
298   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_3] Reconfigure returned %d", result);
299   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
300     return result;
301   }
302 
303   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_4] Converting ASCII to YUV420");
304   h265_encoder_ascii_to_yuv420(ascii_data, width, height, encoder->yuv_buf);
305   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_5] YUV420 conversion done");
306 
307   // Setup frame data pointers
308   encoder->frame->data[0] = encoder->yuv_buf;
309   encoder->frame->data[1] = encoder->yuv_buf + width * height;
310   encoder->frame->data[2] = encoder->yuv_buf + width * height + (width / 2) * (height / 2);
311   encoder->frame->linesize[0] = width;
312   encoder->frame->linesize[1] = width / 2;
313   encoder->frame->linesize[2] = width / 2;
314   encoder->frame->width = width;
315   encoder->frame->height = height;
316   encoder->frame->format = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
317 
318   // Request keyframe if needed
319   if (encoder->request_keyframe) {
320     encoder->frame->pict_type = AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I;
321     encoder->request_keyframe = false;
322   }
323 
324   // Encode frame
325   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_6] Sending frame to encoder: %ux%u format=%d", width, height, encoder->frame->format);
326   int send_ret = avcodec_send_frame(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->frame);
327   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_7] avcodec_send_frame returned %d", send_ret);
328   if (send_ret < 0) {
329     log_error("H265_ENCODE: avcodec_send_frame failed: %d", send_ret);
330     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to send frame to encoder");
331   }
332 
333   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_8] Receiving encoded packet...");
334   int recv_ret = avcodec_receive_packet(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->packet);
335   if (recv_ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
336     // EAGAIN is normal - encoder needs more frames before outputting a packet
337     log_dev("H265_ENCODE: EAGAIN - encoder buffering frames (normal), waiting for next frame");
338     *output_size = 0; // No output this time
339     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
340   }
341   if (recv_ret < 0) {
342     log_error("H265_ENCODE: avcodec_receive_packet failed: %d (EAGAIN=%d)", recv_ret, AVERROR(EAGAIN));
343     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive encoded packet");
344   }
345   log_debug("H265_ENCODE: Got packet: size=%d", (int)encoder->packet->size);
346 
347   uint8_t flags = 0;
348   if (encoder->frame->pict_type == AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I) {
349     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME;
350     encoder->keyframes++;
351   }
352 
353   if (width != encoder->current_width || height != encoder->current_height) {
354     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE;
355   }
356 
357   size_t header_size = 5;
358   size_t packet_size = encoder->packet->size;
359   size_t required_size = header_size + packet_size;
360 
361   if (required_size > *output_size) {
362     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu", required_size, *output_size);
363   }
364 
365   // Write header: [flags][width:u16][height:u16]
366   output_buf[0] = flags;
367   output_buf[1] = (width >> 8) & 0xFF;
368   output_buf[2] = width & 0xFF;
369   output_buf[3] = (height >> 8) & 0xFF;
370   output_buf[4] = height & 0xFF;
371 
372   // Write encoded data
373   if (packet_size > 0) {
374     memcpy(output_buf + header_size, encoder->packet->data, packet_size);
375   }
376 
377   *output_size = required_size;
378   encoder->total_frames++;
379   log_dev("[H265_ENCODE_DONE] Encoding complete, output_size=%zu", required_size);
380 
381   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
382 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE,
    H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME, H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE,
    log_debug, log_dev, log_error, log_info, and
    SET_ERRNO.






h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t
  initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)


Create a new x265 encoder for ASCII frames




Parameters


initial_width Initial frame width (characters)


initial_height Initial frame height (characters)


Returns


Encoder handle, or NULL on error


Definition at line 48 of file encoder.c.


48                                                                                      {
49   if (initial_width == 0 || initial_height == 0) {
50     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Frame dimensions must be non-zero");
51     return NULL;
52   }
53 
54   h265_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(h265_encoder_t), h265_encoder_t *);
55   if (!enc) {
56     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate encoder structure");
57     return NULL;
58   }
59 
60   // Find best available HEVC encoder (try by ID first, then by name for specific implementations)
61   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder(AV_CODEC_ID_HEVC);
62 
63   // If generic HEVC encoder not found, try specific implementations by name
64   if (!codec) {
65     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("libx265");
66   }
67   if (!codec) {
68     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_vaapi");
69   }
70   if (!codec) {
71     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_videotoolbox");
72   }
73   if (!codec) {
74     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_nvenc");
75   }
76   if (!codec) {
77     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_qsv");
78   }
79 
80   if (!codec) {
81     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "HEVC encoder not found");
82     SAFE_FREE(enc);
83     return NULL;
84   }
85 
86   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
87   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
88     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate codec context");
89     SAFE_FREE(enc);
90     return NULL;
91   }
92 
93   // Configure codec context
94   enc->codec_ctx->width = initial_width;
95   enc->codec_ctx->height = initial_height;
96   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
97   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, 30};
98   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){30, 1};
99 
100   // Set ultrafast preset for low-latency encoding
101   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "preset", "ultrafast", 0) < 0) {
102     log_warn("Failed to set preset to ultrafast, using default");
103   }
104   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "tune", "zerolatency", 0) < 0) {
105     log_warn("Failed to set tune to zerolatency, using default");
106   }
107 
108   enc->codec_ctx->thread_count = h265_thread_count();
109 
110   // Defer avcodec_open2 to first encode call. The initial resolution (800x600) is
111   // usually wrong — the actual frame resolution is only known when the first capture
112   // arrives. Opening the codec here would create an x265 thread pool (~1s) that gets
113   // immediately discarded on reconfigure, wasting startup time.
114   enc->codec_opened = false;
115 
116   // Allocate frame and packet
117   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
118   enc->packet = av_packet_alloc();
119 
120   if (!enc->frame || !enc->packet) {
121     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame/packet");
122     if (enc->frame)
123       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
124     if (enc->packet)
125       av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
126     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
127     SAFE_FREE(enc);
128     return NULL;
129   }
130 
131   enc->current_width = initial_width;
132   enc->current_height = initial_height;
133 
134   // Allocate YUV420P buffer (Y plane + U/V planes)
135   size_t stride = initial_width;
136   enc->yuv_buf_size = stride * initial_height * 3 / 2;
137   enc->yuv_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(enc->yuv_buf_size, uint8_t *);
138   if (!enc->yuv_buf) {
139     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate YUV buffer");
140     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
141     av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
142     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
143     SAFE_FREE(enc);
144     return NULL;
145   }
146 
147   log_info("FFmpeg HEVC encoder created: %ux%u (codec: %s)", initial_width, initial_height, codec->name);
148 
149   // Register encoder for debugging
150   NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER(enc, "h265_encoder", NULL);
151 
152   return enc;
153 }






References ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, and
    SET_ERRNO.






void
  h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Destroy an x265 encoder




Parameters


encoder Encoder to destroy (may be NULL)


Definition at line 155 of file encoder.c.


155                                                    {
156   if (!encoder)
157     return;
158 
159   if (encoder->frame) {
160     av_frame_free(&encoder->frame);
161   }
162   if (encoder->packet) {
163     av_packet_free(&encoder->packet);
164   }
165   if (encoder->codec_ctx) {
166     // Capture x265 encoder cleanup output (final frame stats, etc.)
167     LOG_IO("hevc", {
168       avcodec_free_context(&encoder->codec_ctx);
169     });
170   }
171   if (encoder->yuv_buf) {
172     SAFE_FREE(encoder->yuv_buf);
173   }
174   SAFE_FREE(encoder);
175 }






References LOG_IO, and SAFE_FREE.






asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush
  (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t *
  output_size)


Flush remaining buffered frames from encoder




Sends a NULL frame to the encoder to flush all buffered frames.
    This should be called before destroying the encoder or when you need to
    ensure all encoded data is retrieved.




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


output_buf Buffer for encoded output


output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual encoded size


Returns


Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ASCIICHAT_OK with
  size=0 when no more data)


USAGE: // After final h265_encode() call, flush remaining
    frames while (size > 0) { size = max_size; h265_encoder_flush(encoder,
    buf, &size); if (size > 0) { // Send the flushed frame... } }


Definition at line 390 of file encoder.c.


390                                                                                                         {
391   if (!encoder || !output_buf || !output_size) {
392     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid encoder or output parameters for flush");
393   }
394 
395   if (*output_size < 5) {
396     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
397   }
398 
399   // Send NULL frame to signal end of stream (flushes encoder)
400   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Sending NULL frame to encoder to flush buffered data");
401   int send_ret = avcodec_send_frame(encoder->codec_ctx, NULL);
402   if (send_ret < 0) {
403     log_error("H265_FLUSH: avcodec_send_frame(NULL) failed: %d", send_ret);
404     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to flush encoder");
405   }
406 
407   // Try to receive a packet
408   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Attempting to receive flushed packet...");
409   int recv_ret = avcodec_receive_packet(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->packet);
410   if (recv_ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
411     log_dev("H265_FLUSH: EAGAIN - no more frames available");
412     *output_size = 0;
413     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
414   }
415   if (recv_ret == AVERROR_EOF) {
416     log_debug("H265_FLUSH: EOF - encoder is flushed");
417     *output_size = 0;
418     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
419   }
420   if (recv_ret < 0) {
421     log_error("H265_FLUSH: avcodec_receive_packet failed: %d", recv_ret);
422     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive flushed packet");
423   }
424 
425   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Got flushed packet: size=%d", (int)encoder->packet->size);
426 
427   // Check if packet size exceeds output buffer
428   size_t header_size = 5;
429   size_t packet_size = encoder->packet->size;
430   size_t required_size = header_size + packet_size;
431 
432   if (required_size > *output_size) {
433     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu", required_size, *output_size);
434   }
435 
436   // Write header: [flags][width:u16][height:u16]
437   uint8_t flags = 0;
438   if (encoder->frame->pict_type == AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I) {
439     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME;
440     encoder->keyframes++;
441   }
442 
443   output_buf[0] = flags;
444   output_buf[1] = (encoder->current_width >> 8) & 0xFF;
445   output_buf[2] = encoder->current_width & 0xFF;
446   output_buf[3] = (encoder->current_height >> 8) & 0xFF;
447   output_buf[4] = encoder->current_height & 0xFF;
448 
449   // Write encoded data
450   if (packet_size > 0) {
451     memcpy(output_buf + header_size, encoder->packet->data, packet_size);
452   }
453 
454   *output_size = required_size;
455   encoder->total_frames++;
456 
457   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
458 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE,
    H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME, log_debug, log_dev,
    log_error, and SET_ERRNO.






void
  h265_encoder_get_stats (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint64_t *
  total_frames, uint64_t * keyframes, uint32_t *
  avg_bitrate)


Get encoder statistics




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


total_frames Output: total frames encoded


keyframes Output: total keyframes encoded


avg_bitrate Output: average bitrate in bits per second


Definition at line 460 of file encoder.c.


461                                                    {
462   if (!encoder)
463     return;
464 
465   if (total_frames)
466     *total_frames = encoder->total_frames;
467   if (keyframes)
468     *keyframes = encoder->keyframes;
469   if (avg_bitrate)
470     *avg_bitrate = encoder->avg_bitrate;
471 }






Referenced by h265_client_get_stats().






void
  h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Request a keyframe on the next encode




Forces the encoder to produce an I-frame (keyframe) on the next
    call to h265_encoder_encode(). Useful for error recovery or stream
    synchronization.




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


Definition at line 384 of file encoder.c.


384                                                             {
385   if (encoder) {
386     encoder->request_keyframe = true;
387   }
388 }
